source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Rowan Reliance SOBM Spill

context.region: Gulf of Mexico - Garden Banks Block 958

event.top_event: Loss of Containment - Unauthorized discharge of synthetic oil base mud
actions_taken: Upper annular was closed and drilling activities were suspended
ROV deployed to investigate loss of SOBM on active system
BOP#2 unlatched and retrieved to surface
Riser joints laid down and torque values recorded
Connection flange separated and inspected on 7 April 2015
event.summary: On 23 March 2015 at 0505 hours, the Rowan Reliance drill ship under contract to Cobalt International Energy reported a discharge of approximately 2200 barrels of 15.3 ppg synthetic oil base mud (SOBM) during well operations on Well 002 (North Platte #2) in Garden Banks Block 958 at 4846 ft water depth. The primary cause was the failure to install a Seal Sub (Mud Pin) in the flange connection between the flex joint termination spool and the first riser joint (Joint #64). Contributing factors included inadequate procedures, insufficient crew handover, failure to recognize the SOBM buildup during ROV coverage, command structure issues, and under-torqued bolts (1250 psi instead of required 1600 psi). Of the 2200 bbl lost, 1557 bbl entered open water and 643 bbl were lost in open hole. Property damage totaled $151,353.
recommendations: BSEE Lafayette District recommends a Safety and Environmental Management Systems (SEMS) audit be conducted concurrently during Cobalt internal audit scheduled for November 2015

hazards.name: Pressurized drilling fluid in riser system

threats.name: Missing Seal Sub (Mud Pin) in flange connection
Failure to detect SOBM accumulation during ROV inspection

consequences.name: Unauthorized discharge of 2200 barrels SOBM to offshore waters
Environmental pollution of Gulf of Mexico
Damage to riser joint and flex joint termination spool

controls.name: Seal Sub (Mud Pin) installation requirement
controls.barrier_role: Physical barrier to prevent fluid migration
controls.lod_basis: Cameron LoadKing 3.5 Flange Riser design specification

supporting_text: the ultimate root cause of the SOBM spill incident was due to the lack of the installation of a Seal Sub (Mud Pin) in between the flex joint termination spool and first riser joint (Joint #64)
BSEE visually verified the absence of the Seal Sub (Mud Pin) during an on-site investigation on 7 April 2015, when the BOP was retrieved to the surface

controls.name: Riser bolt torque specification and application
controls.lod_basis: Cameron LoadKing 3.5 Flange Riser and Riser Connection Operations & Maintenance Manual

supporting_text: Rowan informed BSEE that when BOP#2 and the riser joints were made up for deployment, the Torq-Lite RT-40 wrench was set at 1250 pounds per square inch (psi) which is equivalent to 13,000 foot-pounds (ft-lb). However, according to the Torq-Lite current operations manual, the torque wrench should have been set to 1600 psi (18,000 ft-lb)

the low torque valves recorded when breaking riser Joint #64 bolts revealed that the bolts were under-torqued when it was made up to the flex joint termination spool during BOP#2 deployment that provided a migration pathway of the SOBM into offshore waters

controls.name: BOP and riser deployment procedures and checklist
controls.barrier_role: Ensure correct assembly and installation of critical components
controls.lod_basis: Rowan procedure RMS-WCG-013 and company operational standards

supporting_text: inadequate Rowan procedures and checklist for deployment of the BOP and riser

Rowan subsea personnel did not recognize or had any previous experience that required the Seal Sub (Mud Pin) to be installed in the 'pin-up' configuration in the flex joint termination spool on the Cameron LoadKing riser system

controls.name: Crew handover procedures during critical operations
controls.barrier_role: Ensure continuity of knowledge and critical task completion
controls.lod_basis: Operational best practice for crew change management

supporting_text: insufficient handover during a crew change of Rowan Subsea personnel during a critical phase of the BOP and riser deployment

controls.name: ROV visual inspection and monitoring of riser system
controls.barrier_role: Detect anomalies and SOBM accumulation early

supporting_text: On 8 March 2015, the remote operated vehicle (ROV) discovered an accumulation of SOBM-like material on the first riser joint (Joint #64) and the flex joint termination spool but was dismissed as drill cuttings that was being discharged overboard

Numerous ROV dives after 8 March 2015 continued to observe the accumulation of SOBM-like material on the first riser joint and termination spool; however, according to information reported to BSEE during monthly rig inspections, Cobalt continued to assume that the SOBM-like material was drill cuttings
Cobalt's failure to recognize what was causing the buildup of SOBM and/or drilling cuttings in the area of the first riser joint and flex joint termination spool throughout the ROV coverage

controls.name: Organizational safety culture and command structure
controls.barrier_role: Enable open communication and alternative explanations
controls.lod_basis: Safety management and organizational culture standards

supporting_text: the culture of top down command structure may have led to misinterpretation of what material was being spilled and it prevented rig personnel for suggesting alternative explanations

controls.name: Operator supervision and oversight of contractor activities
controls.barrier_role: Ensure contractor compliance and identify emerging issues

supporting_text: Cobalt failed to provide adequate supervision during well operations on Well #002 (North Platte #2)
Cobalt failed to recognize throughout the ROV coverage what was causing the buildup of SOBM in the area of the first riser joint and flex joint termination spool

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
